reasoning: Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi stated in late 2024 that it was "dumb to IPO" and they were waiting until at least 2025. By January 2025, Databricks raised an unprecedented $10B-$15B in equity and debt financing, which multiple reports cited as a move specifically designed to delay an IPO until market conditions are optimal. There have been no reports in 2025 of Databricks hiring investment bankers or underwriters for a near-term IPO, nor have there been credible rumors of a confidential S-1 filing. An IPO roadmap typically involves hiring bankers several months in advance, followed by a confidential S-1 submission before any public filing. Given less than six weeks remain until the July 1, 2025 deadline and Databricks is extremely well-capitalized, the probability of them announcing an IPO by the target date is exceptionally low.
